methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 11 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Credo Semiconductor
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: Ouster
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Public), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Aeva Technologies is a publicly traded semiconductor company founded by Soroush Salehian, currently valued at $1B+ after raising $660M+. The valuation assessment draws from a peer universe of 11 same-sector companies across similar valuation tiers, comprising 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms including Etched, SambaNova Systems, Credo Semiconductor, Tenstorrent, and Rebellions.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, as Aeva's public status and disclosed valuation allow direct comparison within the semiconductors sector. The 11-company peer set yields a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 24.4x across the 3 public tickers) cannot be applied given the absence of filed revenue data for the target. The peer-median therefore serves as the sole quantifiable anchor.

Analysis supports a valuation range of $1.7B to $2.3B, with a median implied value of $2.0B. This aligns with Aeva's current $1B+ valuation and reflects positioning within the upper-middle band of the peer set. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory and near-term unit economics relative to the heterogeneous peer cohort, which spans early-stage ventures (Etched at Series A, $5.0B) to established public entities. The limited methodological convergence underscores reliance on peer comps as the primary valuation signal.
